            if gcd(nums[i], nums[j]) == p:

def solve(nums: list[int], p: int, queries: list[list[int]]) -> int:

function gcd(a, b) {
    while (b) {
        [a, b] = [b, a % b];
    }
    return Math.abs(a);
}

function check_good_subsequence(nums, p, n) {
    // Check Length 1
    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (nums[i] === p) {
            // Length 1 < N since N >= 2 is guaranteed.
            return true;
        }
    }
    
    // If N=2, we are done because length 2 is not strictly less than N.
    if (n <= 2) {
        return false;
    }

    // Check Length 2 (only needed if N >= 3)
    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        for (let j = i + 1; j < n; j++) {
            if (gcd(nums[i], nums[j]) === p) {
                // Length 2 < N since N >= 3.
                return true;
            }
        }
    }

    // If we assume existence implies a small subsequence exists, we return false here.
    // If we had to check all subsequences, this is where the hard part is.
    return false;
